title: Intra-Epidermal Fiber Density Determination of Rodent Foot Pad Biopsies
author: Eva Feldman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7rehm3e

steps.title: Following perfusion, remove entire foot pad...
steps.instruction: Following perfusion, remove entire foot pad with a sharp razor blade. Begin by cutting skin between the plantar surface of the foot and the toes, then start at the heel to gently remove the skin and superficial fascia of the foot. Be careful to only handle the tissue by the heel.

steps.title: Rinse in graded sucrose 5 –...
steps.instruction: Rinse in graded sucrose 5 – 20%, minimum 12 hours in each

steps.title: Remove foot and fix overnight (12-18 hours) in 4% paraformaldehyde
steps.instruction: Remove foot and fix overnight (12-18 hours) in 4% paraformaldehyde

steps.title: Remove skin and process as described above
steps.instruction: Remove skin and process as described above.

steps.title: Follow IHC protocol using rabbit anti-PGP9
steps.instruction: Follow IHC protocol using rabbit anti-PGP9.5, 1:1000 followed by AlexaFluor 598, 1:200 (Molecular Probes). Apply coverslips with ProLong antifade kit (Molecular Probes)

steps.title: Imaging
steps.instruction: Imaging: Three images per sample are collected on an Olympus FluoView 500 confocal microscope using a 60 X 1.2 water immersion objective at a resolution of 800 X 600 pixels. The optical section thickness is 0.5 um. Forty images per stack are flattened using MetaMorph (version 6.14) arithmetic option. Integrated morphometry analysis is used to exclude extraneous signals. The data are presented as the percent area of PGP9.5 positive fibers per area of epidermis.
